seoTitle: Port Kembla vs Port Stephens — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Kembla and Port Stephens. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Kembla vs Port Stephens: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Kembla and Port Stephens.
heading: Port Kembla vs Port Stephens — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Kembla, New South Wales and Port Stephens, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Kembla has a lower overall climate risk score (43/100) compared to Port Stephens (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Kembla or Port Stephens safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Kembla has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Port Stephens scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Kembla scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Port Stephens scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Kembla or Port Stephens?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Kembla has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
